source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/00_기타 작업 서류/240718 - DK 제품별 자료 정리/DK_제품별 자료 정리(pdf)/4. A1417MCW/6. IEC Test Report (EMC, Safety)/PRJ0042257 REP028549 CBTR IEC60601-1 H&abyz A1717MCW (Original)_CSV.pdf

document.title: PRJ0042257 REP028549 CBTR IEC60601-1 H&abyz A1717MCW (Original)

sourceSummary.oneLine: IEC 60601-1 safety test report excerpt covering mains parts, power cords, mechanical hazards, radiation hazards, temperature, and fire prevention for a power supply unit.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory safety evidence for IEC 60601-1 compliance, with several component-level items requiring evaluation in the final end product application.
keyPoints: The appliance inlet is used as the mains disconnecting device.
Certified appliance coupler is provided and referenced to appended Table 8.10.
Mains fuses are provided, protective devices have adequate breaking capacity, and no fuse is placed in the protective earth conductor.
Mechanical hazards such as moving parts, trapping zones, emergency stops, patient constraints, pressure vessels, and support systems are largely not applicable to the power supply unit.
Temperature and fire prevention clauses show passing results, including no thermal cut-outs and suitable fire enclosure-related construction.

entities.name: Report No. 269183
entities.evidence: Pages 48-69 identify the document as Report No. 269183 under IEC 60601-1.

entities.name: A1717MCW
entities.evidence: The source path names the file as an IEC60601-1 CBTR for A1717MCW.

entities.name: A1417MCW
entities.evidence: The source path places the report under the product folder '4. A1417MCW'.

entities.name: IEC 60601-1
entities.evidence: Each page header in the chunk states 'IEC 60601-1'.

entities.name: TRF No. IEC60601_1J
entities.evidence: The clause tables are labeled 'TRF No. IEC60601_1J'.

entities.name: ISO 14971
entities.evidence: Multiple risk management file entries cite ISO 14971 clauses, including 'ISO 14971 Cl. 4.2-4.4, 5, 6.2-6.5'.

concepts.name: Mains isolation by appliance inlet
concepts.evidence: Clause 8.11.1 states the appliance inlet is used as the mains disconnecting device.

concepts.name: Certified appliance coupler
concepts.evidence: Clause 8.11.3.4 records 'Certified appliance coupler provided' and verdict P.

concepts.name: Mains fuse protection
concepts.evidence: Clause 8.11.5 states 'Mains fuses provided' and protective devices have adequate breaking capacity.

concepts.name: Component-level end product evaluation
concepts.evidence: Several clauses state the equipment is a power supply unit and shall be additionally evaluated in the end product application.

concepts.name: Fire enclosure material flammability
concepts.evidence: Clause 11.3 records internal wires suitably rated and PCB material rated minimum V-1 flammability.

evidence.claim: Internal wiring and components were judged unlikely to be damaged during assembly or service.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.10.5 b: 'No wiring or components likely to be damaged during assembly or service' and 'No risks identified related to wiring protection'; verdict P.

evidence.claim: The appliance inlet is the mains disconnecting device for the equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.11.1 a states 'The appliance inlet is used as mains disconnecting device'; verdict P.

evidence.claim: A fuse or semiconductor device is not used as an isolating means.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.11.1 g states 'Not used as isolating device'; verdict P.

evidence.claim: The power supply cord set was not evaluated in this equipment/report.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 8.11.3.1 through 8.11.3.3 state the power supply cord set is not evaluated in this equipment or report; verdict N/A.

evidence.claim: The equipment uses a certified appliance coupler.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.11.3.4 states 'Certified appliance coupler provided. See appended Table 8.10'; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Mains fuses are provided, and protective devices have adequate breaking capacity.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.11.5 states 'Mains fuses provided' and 'Protective devices have adequate breaking capacity to interrupt the max. fault current'; verdict P.

evidence.claim: No fuse is provided in the protective earth conductor.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.11.5 states 'No fuse in protective earth conductor'; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Internal mains-part wiring and PCB tracks were considered suitable.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 8.11.6 states 'Suitable conductors used' and 'Suitable wiring and tracks on PCB used'; verdict P.

evidence.claim: Mechanical moving-part hazards were generally not applicable because the evaluated equipment is a power supply unit.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 9.2.1 states the equipment is a power supply unit and shall be additionally evaluated in the end product application; moving-parts risk items are N/A.

evidence.claim: No emergency stopping devices are provided.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 9.2.4 states 'No emergency stopping devices'; verdict N/A.

evidence.claim: No patient constraint parts are present for quick-release evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 9.2.5 states 'No patient constraints parts'; verdict N/A.

evidence.claim: The equipment does not produce X-radiation.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 10.1.1 states 'Equipment does not produce X-radiation'; verdict N/A.

evidence.claim: The equipment passed excessive temperature requirements for ME equipment parts, with details in appended Tables 11.1.1 and 11.1.3.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 11.1.1 states 'See appended Table 11.1.1 and 11.1.3' and notes the power supply unit requires end product application evaluation; verdict P.

evidence.claim: No thermal cut-outs are present in the equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 11.1.1 states 'No thermal cut-outs in the equipment'; verdict P.

evidence.claim: The enclosure was considered suitable for component power supply fire prevention, subject to end product fire-hazard evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 11.2.1 states 'Suitable enclosure provided for component power supply as a component when installed in the end product' and fire hazard shall be additionally evaluated; verdict P.

evidence.claim: The equipment is not intended for use in oxygen rich environments.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 11.2.2.1 states 'Not intended used in conjunction with oxygen rich environments'; verdict N/A.

evidence.claim: Internal wires and PCB material met fire enclosure flammability expectations.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 11.3 states 'Internal wires suitably rated' and 'PCB material is rated as minimum V-1 of flammability'; verdict P.

evidence.claim: There are no openings in the bottom below the fire enclosure.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 11.3 b 1 states 'No openings in bottom below fire enclosure'; verdict P.

reviewFlags.reason: The chunk repeatedly states that the evaluated equipment is a power supply unit and certain hazards shall be additionally evaluated in the end product application.
Document date and revision/version are not visible in this chunk.
